frame_analyses.description: Two laparoscopic scissors positioned at gauze with visible 4cm diameter black circle pattern marked with red dots
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Gauze appears properly suspended between clamps, clear pattern visible

frame_analyses.description: Left scissors initiating cut at top of circle pattern, right scissors providing counter-traction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination, cut starting at 12 o'clock position

frame_analyses.description: Scissors progressing clockwise around circle, approximately at 3 o'clock position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady cutting motion, maintaining proximity to marked line

frame_analyses.description: Cutting continues at 6 o'clock position, small deviation visible from marked line
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Some imprecision noted at bottom of circle

frame_analyses.description: Cutting at 9 o'clock position, returning toward starting point
Final connection made, circular pattern cut complete with instruments withdrawing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion of circle, gauze remains attached to clamps

penalties.description: Deviation at 6 o'clock position approximately 1.5mm outside marked line

confidence_rationale: Camera angle provides good view of cutting path, but some deviation measurements are estimates due to perspective. Time measurement clear from instrument appearance to task completion.
cannot_determine: Exact mm measurements at all 8 cardinal points due to camera angle limitations
technique_summary: Expert-level performance with excellent time (57s vs 60-90s benchmark) and minimal deviation penalties. Smooth bimanual coordination throughout cutting sequence.
strengths: Excellent completion time well within expert range
Consistent cutting technique with good instrument coordination
improvement_suggestions: Focus on precision at 6 o'clock position to minimize deviation
Consider slightly slower cutting speed at curve transitions
